title: The Great Conversation: A Reader's Guide to the Great Books of the Western World
excerpt: Mortimer Adler's introduction to the Great Books of the Western World series, explaining the concept of the Great Conversation — the ongoing dialogue across centuries about the fundamental questions of human existence.
keyIdeas: The Great Books constitute an ongoing conversation across centuries about enduring human questions
Western civilization is unified by a tradition of thought that transcends historical periods
Reading the Great Books is essential to becoming an educated person in the Western tradition
keyTakeaways: The Great Conversation is the dialogue among authors across centuries about fundamental ideas
The Great Books are those that have the most to contribute to this conversation
Reading the Great Books in conversation with each other deepens understanding of each
The Syntopicon indexes ideas across all the Great Books, revealing patterns of thought
Liberal education is incomplete without direct engagement with the Great Books
whoShouldRead: Anyone embarking on a Great Books reading program
Students of liberal education and the humanities
Readers of How to Read a Book seeking the next step
Those interested in the philosophy of education
whoShouldSkip: Readers who reject the Western canon as a framework
Those looking for diverse or non-Western intellectual traditions
People who prefer specialized study to broad reading programs
